feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: Optimal Transport with Point-wise Constraints
entry.content:
It holds $$ |\overline{\mathbf{L}}| = \sum_{\mathcal{T} \in \text{ST}(\overline{\W})} \prod_{ij} \mathcal{T}_{ij} $$.

where $$\text{ST}(\overline{\W})$$ is the set of spanning trees of $$\overline{\W}$$.

Relying on the above matrix tree theorem for weighted graphs, one can easily compute the determinant of graph Laplacians on $\mathcal{S}_W$ as follows.

For $\W \in \mathcal{S}_W$, $ \overline{\bm{L}} = \prod_{r=1}^R \gamma(r) $ where $\gamma(r)$ is the set of edges in the unique cycle associated to the $r^{th}$ connected component of $\W$.

One can notice that a connected component of $\overline{\W}$ is a spanning tree with one cycle (CRSF). In the below derivation, equation \ref{cycle_trick} is derived by noticing that to obtain a spanning tree from a CRSF, one only has to remove an edge from the cycle. \begin{align} |\overline{\bm{L}}| &= \sum_{\mathcal{T} \in \text{ST}(\Tilde{\W})} \prod_{ij} \mathcal{T}_{ij}
&= \prod_{ij} \overline{W}_{ij} \prod_{r=1}^R \sum_{i’ j’ \in \gamma(r)} \overline{W}_{i’j’}^{-1} \label{cycle_trick}
&= \prod_{r=1}^R |\gamma(r)| \quad \text{for unitary graph } \W \in \mathcal{S}_{\scaleto{W}{4pt}} :. \end{align}

$\mathcal{C}(\W)$ can be computed with linear complexity using \cref{algo:compute_C(w)}.

This theme supports rendering beautiful math in inline and display modes using MathJax 3 engine. You just need to surround your math expression with $$, like $$ E = mc^2 $$. If you leave it inside a paragraph, it will produce an inline expression, just like \(E = mc^2\).

To use display mode, again surround your expression with $$ and place it as a separate paragraph. Here is an example:

\[\sum_{k=1}^\infty |\langle x, e_k \rangle|^2 \leq \|x\|^2\]

You can also use \begin{equation}...\end{equation} instead of $$ for display mode math. MathJax will automatically number equations:

\begin{equation} \label{eq:cauchy-schwarz} \left( \sum_{k=1}^n a_k b_k \right)^2 \leq \left( \sum_{k=1}^n a_k^2 \right) \left( \sum_{k=1}^n b_k^2 \right) \end{equation}

and by adding \label{...} inside the equation environment, we can now refer to the equation using \eqref.

Note that MathJax 3 is a major re-write of MathJax that brought a significant improvement to the loading and rendering speed, which is now on par with KaTeX.

entry.title: Displaying External Posts on Your al-folio Blog
